user.title: Belgian Water Prices
user.description: Enter your postcode and we'll match you to the right water utility. If we can't, you'll pick from a list on the next step.

manual.title: Pick your water utility
manual.description: We could not resolve a utility for that postcode. Pick yours below.

reconfigure.title: Update your postcode
reconfigure.description: Enter the new postcode for this entry. We'll re-resolve the utility and reload the integration. Annual consumption, registered residents, social tariff and the water meter sensor are kept; the saved commune is cleared if the resolved utility changes (the new utility uses different commune IDs).

reconfigure_manual.title: Pick your water utility
reconfigure_manual.description: We could not resolve a utility for that postcode. Pick yours below.

options.title: Annual consumption
options.description: Your annual water consumption in cubic metres. Used by the projected-cost sensor; defaults to 80 m³/year. Flemish utilities also need the number of registered residents (drives the basis volume) and an opt-in for the VMM social tariff. Optionally point at a water meter sensor (cumulative m³, device_class=water) so the year-to-date cost sensor can read consumption since 1 January from Home Assistant's recorder.

data.commune: Commune (per-commune utilities only: De Watergroep / Farys / Pidpa / Water-link)
data.water_meter_sensor: Water meter sensor (optional override; auto-detected from the Energy dashboard if blank)

abort.already_configured: This water utility is already configured.

data.commune: Commune (per-commune utilities only: De Watergroep / Farys / Pidpa / Water-link)
data.water_meter_sensor: Water meter sensor (optional override; auto-detected from the Energy dashboard if blank)

backfill_prices.name: Backfill price history
backfill_prices.description: Import flat-line hourly long-term-statistics rows for this integration's price sensors so the History and Energy dashboards show a tariff line going back further than the install date.

entry_id.name: Config entry
entry_id.description: Optional. The config entry to backfill. Leave empty to backfill every loaded entry.

start_date.name: Start date
start_date.description: Optional. Earliest date to backfill. Defaults to 1 January of the current year. Clamped to the tariff snapshot's valid_from.

clear.name: Clear before importing
clear.description: If on, wipe the existing long-term statistics for the targeted sensors before importing. Off by default.

init.title: Retry the {utility} tariff fetch
init.description:
The last successfully fetched {utility} tariff is {age_days} days old (valid until {valid_until}). Last fetch error: {last_error}.

Press Submit to trigger an immediate refresh. The Repair card auto-clears if the next fetch returns a fresh snapshot; otherwise it stays so you can retry again or investigate the underlying failure (check the integration's GitHub issues or the daily live-check workflow run).
